docs.title: ffmt
docs.text: A fast, configurable Fortran formatter with support for Fypp, Doxygen, and OpenACC/OpenMP directives. Written in Rust.

docs.title: Code formatting
docs.text:
• Indentation -- scope-based indentation for all Fortran constructs
  • Whitespace -- consistent spacing around operators, commas, colons, parentheses
  • Keywords -- case normalization (IF -> if, ENDDO -> end do)
  • Named ends -- adds procedure/module name to bare end statements
  • Continuation lines -- normalized with proportional re-indentation
  • Line wrapping -- string-aware rewrapping of long lines (never breaks inside string literals)
  • Trailing semicolons -- removed (Fortitude S081)
  • Double-colon enforcement -- adds :: to declarations missing it (integer x -> integer :: x)
  • Relational operator modernization -- converts legacy operators (.eq. -> ==, .ne. -> /=, etc.)
  • Multi-statement splitting -- splits x = 1; y = 2 onto separate lines (opt-in)
  • Assignment alignment -- aligns = across consecutive assignments (opt-in)
  • Use-statement reformatting -- one import per line for use, only: (opt-in)
  • EOL normalization -- normalize line endings to LF or CRLF

docs.title: Comment formatting
docs.text:
• Comment rewrapping -- joins and re-wraps !/!! comment blocks to fill line width
  • Inline comment spacing -- enforces 2+ spaces before inline ! comments (Fortitude S102)
  • !< alignment -- aligns inline Doxygen comments within declaration groups (2-space minimum)
  • Comment space -- ensures space after ! in regular comments
  • !& cleanup -- strips trailing !& no-op comments that cause Cray ftn errors
  • & ! cleanup -- converts comment-only continuation lines to standalone comments
  • Format suppression -- ! ffmt off / ! ffmt on to skip formatting for code regions

docs.title: Structural formatting
docs.text:
• Blank lines after openers -- removes blank lines after module, subroutine, if...then, do, #ifdef, #:if, etc.
  • Blank lines before closers -- removes blank lines before end, #endif, #else, else, case, etc.
  • Blank lines after block ends -- ensures blank line after end subroutine/end function before next block
  • Declaration separation -- ensures blank line between declarations and executable code
  • :: alignment -- vertically aligns :: in consecutive declaration blocks
  • Declaration compaction -- removes blank lines between consecutive declarations
  • Use compaction -- removes blank lines between consecutive use statements
  • Continuation & alignment -- align trailing & at column limit (opt-in)

docs.title: Preprocessor support
docs.text:
• Fypp -- #:if, #:for, #:call, #:def, $:, @: macros handled correctly
  • C preprocessor -- #ifdef, #ifndef, #if, #else, #endif preserved
  • OpenACC -- !$acc directives preserved
  • OpenMP -- !$omp directives preserved

docs.title: Comparison with other formatters
docs.text: Formatter Language Status Notes fprettify Python Unmaintained (last release 2020) Free-form only. Fypp support. Requires multiple passes for convergence. findent C Active Indentation and fixed/free conversion. No whitespace normalization. Codee Formatter Proprietary Active Commercial. Tree-sitter based. Fixed and free-form. LFortran fmt Rust In development AST-based. Part of the LFortran compiler project. Fortitude Rust Active Linter with auto-fix, not a full formatter. Tree-sitter based. f90-mode Emacs Lisp Active Emacs built-in. Indentation only.

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text:
- uses: sbryngelson/ffmt@latest
  with:
    args: "--check src/"

docs.title: pre-commit
docs.text:
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/sbryngelson/ffmt
    rev: v0.2.8
    hooks:
      - id: ffmt

To auto-update to the latest version:

pre-commit autoupdate

Or use pre-commit.ci to update hooks automatically via pull requests.

docs.title: GitLab CI
docs.text:
format:
  image: python:3.12
  script:
    - pip install ffmt
    - ffmt --check src/

docs.title: Generic CI
docs.text:
pip install ffmt
ffmt --check src/

The --check flag exits with code 1 if any files would be changed, making it suitable for CI gates. Use --diff to see what would change:

ffmt --diff src/

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Create ffmt.toml in your project root, or add [tool.ffmt] to pyproject.toml. Config is searched upward from each formatted file's directory.

Tip

Use ffmt --dump-config to see the resolved configuration for the current directory.

docs.title: Toggle values
docs.text:
Most options accept three values:

Value Meaning true Actively apply the rule false Actively suppress the rule "preserve" Don't touch -- leave as-is

docs.title: indent-width (integer)
docs.text:
Number of spaces per indentation level.

Default: 4

! indent-width = 2              ! indent-width = 4
subroutine s_foo()              subroutine s_foo()
  if (.true.) then                  if (.true.) then
    x = 1                              x = 1
  end if                            end if
end subroutine                  end subroutine

docs.title: line-length (integer)
docs.text:
Maximum line length. Code and comments are wrapped at this limit. Set to 1000 to effectively disable wrapping.

Default: 132

docs.title: keyword-case ("lower" | "upper" | "preserve")
docs.text:
Case normalization for Fortran keywords.

Default: "lower"

! "lower"                       ! "upper"
if (x == 1) then                IF (x == 1) THEN
    call s_foo()                    CALL s_foo()
end if                          END IF

docs.title: normalize-keywords (toggle)
docs.text:
Split compound keywords into their canonical two-word form.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
end do                          enddo
end if                          endif
end subroutine s_foo            endsubroutine s_foo

docs.title: named-ends (toggle)
docs.text:
Add procedure/module name to bare end statements.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
end subroutine s_foo            end subroutine
end function f_bar              end function
end module m_baz                end module

docs.title: enforce-double-colon (toggle)
docs.text:
Add :: separator to variable declarations that are missing it.

Default: true

! true                          ! false (preserve)
integer :: x                    integer x
real(wp) :: y                   real(wp) y
type(foo) :: bar                type(foo) bar

Does not modify function/subroutine signatures (logical function f_foo is left alone).

docs.title: modernize-operators (toggle)
docs.text:
Convert legacy Fortran relational operators to modern symbolic form. String-aware -- does not modify operators inside string literals or comments.

Default: true

! true                          ! false (preserve)
if (x == 1) then                if (x .eq. 1) then
if (y /= 0) then               if (y .ne. 0) then
if (z < 10) then               if (z .lt. 10) then
if (w >= 5) then               if (w .ge. 5) then
s = 'x .eq. y'                 s = 'x .eq. y'   ! strings preserved

docs.title: split-statements (toggle)
docs.text:
Split semicolon-separated statements onto separate lines. The private; public :: idiom is preserved.

Default: false

! true                          ! false (preserve)
x = 1                           x = 1; y = 2; z = 3
y = 2
z = 3
private; public :: s_foo        private; public :: s_foo  ! preserved

docs.title: rewrap-comments (toggle)
docs.text:
Re-wrap long ! and !! comment blocks at line-length. Doxygen !> / !! blocks are joined and re-wrapped as units. Short consecutive comment lines are merged.

Default: true

! true (line-length = 80)
! This is a long comment that was originally on a single very long line but has
! been wrapped to fit within the line length limit.

! false (preserve)
! This is a long comment that was originally on a single very long line but has been wrapped to fit within the line length limit.

docs.title: rewrap-code (toggle)
docs.text:
Re-wrap long code lines at line-length using token-aware splitting. Never breaks inside string literals.

Default: true

! true
call s_long_subroutine_name(arg1, arg2, arg3, &
                            & arg4, arg5)

! false (preserve)
call s_long_subroutine_name(arg1, arg2, arg3, arg4, arg5)

docs.title: align-declarations (boolean)
docs.text:
Vertically align :: in consecutive declaration blocks.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
integer                :: x     integer :: x
real(wp), dimension(3) :: y     real(wp), dimension(3) :: y
logical                :: z     logical :: z

docs.title: align-comments (boolean)
docs.text:
Vertically align !< inline Doxygen comments in declaration blocks. Enforces a minimum 2-space gap before the comment.

Default: true

! true
integer :: x  !< First variable
real(wp) :: y !< Second variable with long type

! becomes:
integer  :: x  !< First variable
real(wp) :: y  !< Second variable with long type

docs.title: align-assignments (toggle)
docs.text:
Align = signs across consecutive assignment statements.

Default: false

! true                          ! false
x        = 1                    x = 1
long_var = 2                    long_var = 2
y        = 3                    y = 3

docs.title: align-ampersand (toggle)
docs.text:
Align trailing & continuation markers at the column limit.

Default: false

! true (line-length = 40)
call s_foo(x,                          &
           & y, z)

! false
call s_foo(x, &
           & y, z)

docs.title: compact-declarations (toggle)
docs.text:
Remove blank lines between consecutive declarations within the same scope.

Default: true

docs.title: compact-use (toggle)
docs.text:
Remove blank lines between consecutive use statements.

Default: true

docs.title: use-formatting (toggle)
docs.text:
Reformat use, only: imports to one item per line. Accepts true, "one-per-line", false, or "preserve".

Default: false

! true / "one-per-line"
use m_foo, only: &
    & s_bar, &
    & s_baz, &
    & f_qux

! false (preserve)
use m_foo, only: s_bar, s_baz, f_qux

docs.title: end-of-line ("lf" | "crlf" | "preserve")
docs.text:
Normalize line endings.

Default: "lf"

docs.title: space-after-comment (toggle)
docs.text:
Ensure a space after ! in regular comments (!comment -> ! comment). Does not affect !$acc, !<, !>, !!, or !&.

Default: true

docs.title: collapse-double-spaces (boolean)
docs.text:
Collapse runs of multiple spaces to a single space in code (not in strings or comments).

Default: true

docs.title: keyword-paren-space (toggle)
docs.text:
Add a space between control-flow keywords and (.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
if (x == 1) then                if(x == 1) then
call s_foo()                    call s_foo()
allocate (x(n))                 allocate(x(n))

docs.title: unicode-to-ascii (boolean)
docs.text:
Replace Unicode Greek letters and math symbols with LaTeX equivalents in comments.

Default: true

docs.title: fypp-list-commas (toggle)
docs.text:
Normalize comma spacing inside Fypp '[...]' list arguments.

Default: true

docs.title: indent-fypp (boolean)
docs.text:
Indent Fypp preprocessor blocks (#:if, #:for, #:call).

Default: true

docs.title: indent-module (boolean)
docs.text:
Indent the body of module and program blocks.

Default: true

docs.title: Whitespace options
docs.text: The [whitespace] section controls spacing around specific operators. All accept true, false, or "preserve".

docs.title: relational (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around ==, /=, <, <=, >, >=.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
if (x == 1) then                if (x==1) then

docs.title: logical (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around .and., .or., .not., .eqv., .neqv..

Default: true

! true                          ! false
if (a .and. b) then             if (a.and.b) then

docs.title: plusminus (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around binary + and -.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
x = a + b - c                  x = a+b-c

docs.title: multdiv (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around * and /.

Default: false

! true                          ! false
x = a * b / c                  x = a*b/c

docs.title: power (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around **.

Default: false

! true                          ! false
x = a ** 2                     x = a**2

docs.title: assignment (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around = in assignments.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
x = 1                           x=1

docs.title: pointer (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around =>.

Default: true

docs.title: concatenation (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around // (string concatenation).

Default: true

! true                          ! false
s = 'hello' // ' world'        s = 'hello'//' world'

docs.title: declaration (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around :: in declarations.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
integer :: x                    integer::x

docs.title: comma (toggle)
docs.text:
Space after ,.

Default: true

! true                          ! false
call s_foo(x, y, z)            call s_foo(x,y,z)

docs.title: slice-colon (toggle)
docs.text:
Space around : in array slices.

Default: false

! true                          ! false
x(1 : n)                       x(1:n)

docs.title: extensions (list of strings)
docs.text:
File extensions to treat as Fortran source.

Default: ["fpp", "f90", "F90", "f95", "f03", "F", "F95", "F03"]

docs.title: exclude (list of strings)
docs.text:
Glob patterns to exclude from formatting.

Default: []

[files]
exclude = ["generated/**", "third_party/**"]

docs.title: respect-gitignore (boolean)
docs.text:
Skip files listed in .gitignore.

Default: true

docs.title: Minimal (MFC-style)
docs.text:
indent-width = 2
line-length = 132
keyword-case = "lower"

docs.title: Strict modernization
docs.text:
indent-width = 4
line-length = 132
keyword-case = "lower"
modernize-operators = true
enforce-double-colon = true
split-statements = true
use-formatting = "one-per-line"

docs.title: Preserve-heavy (minimal changes)
docs.text:
keyword-case = "preserve"
normalize-keywords = "preserve"
rewrap-comments = "preserve"
rewrap-code = "preserve"
modernize-operators = false
enforce-double-colon = false

[whitespace]
relational = "preserve"
logical = "preserve"
plusminus = "preserve"
assignment = "preserve"

docs.title: pyproject.toml
docs.text:
[tool.ffmt]
indent-width = 4
line-length = 132
keyword-case = "lower"

[tool.ffmt.whitespace]
relational = true
multdiv = false

docs.title: Editor Integration
docs.text: ffmt includes a built-in LSP server for real-time format-on-save.

docs.title: LSP server
docs.text: ffmt --lsp

docs.title: VS Code
docs.text:
Add to your workspace settings:

{
    "fortran.formatting.formatter": "ffmt",
    "fortran.formatting.args": ["--stdin-filepath", "${file}", "-"]
}

docs.title: Format on save
docs.text: autocmd BufWritePost *.fpp,*.f90 silent !ffmt %

docs.title: As formatprg
docs.text: set formatprg=ffmt\ -

docs.title: Neovim LSP
docs.text:
vim.lsp.start({
    name = "ffmt",
    cmd = { "ffmt", "--lsp" },
    root_dir = vim.fs.dirname(
        vim.fs.find({ "ffmt.toml", "pyproject.toml" }, { upward = true })[1]
    ),
})

docs.title: Emacs
docs.text:
(defun ffmt-buffer ()
  "Format the current buffer with ffmt."
  (interactive)
  (let ((tmpfile (make-temp-file "ffmt" nil ".fpp")))
    (write-region nil nil tmpfile)
    (shell-command (format "ffmt %s" tmpfile))
    (revert-buffer t t t)
    (delete-file tmpfile)))

(add-hook 'f90-mode-hook
          (lambda () (add-hook 'before-save-hook #'ffmt-buffer nil t)))

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pipCargoFrom source 
pip install ffmt

cargo install ffmt

git clone https://github.com/sbryngelson/ffmt
cd ffmt
cargo install --path .

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
ffmt src/                        # format in-place
ffmt --check src/                # CI mode (exit 1 if changes needed)
ffmt --diff src/                 # show colored diff
ffmt -j 8 src/                   # parallel
cat file.fpp | ffmt -            # stdin/stdout
ffmt --range 10:50 file.fpp      # format only lines 10-50
ffmt --no-cache src/             # ignore file modification cache

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
ffmt works out of the box with no configuration. Just run it on your source directory:

ffmt src/

To check formatting without modifying files (useful for CI):

ffmt --check src/

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Create ffmt.toml in your project root, or add [tool.ffmt] to pyproject.toml. See Configuration for all options.

Most projects need no config file -- the defaults are designed for modern Fortran with Fypp preprocessing.

docs.title: Format suppression
docs.text:
Disable formatting for specific code regions:

! ffmt off
x    =    1       ! preserved exactly as written
y=2;z=3
! ffmt on
w = 4              ! formatted normally

docs.title: What gets preserved
docs.text:
ffmt never modifies:

  • Code between ! ffmt off and ! ffmt on markers
  • String literals and inline expressions (${...}$, @{...}@)
  • Doxygen comment structure (!<, !>, !! associations)
  • Continuation line structure across #ifdef/#:if preprocessor blocks
  • #ifdef/#endif blocks (no indentation change)
  • Fypp/Python code inside $: and @: macro invocations
  • !$acc and !$omp directive lines
